hook: AI gave you a 47-bullet-point action plan when all you needed was one clear decision. Sound familiar?
caption:
AI gave you a 47-bullet-point action plan when all you needed was one clear decision. Sound familiar?

Here's what's actually happening:

ChatGPT doesn't know if you're a "show me everything and I'll filter" person or a "give me the one right answer" person.

So it defaults to MORE. More options. More caveats. More "on the other hand."

For some people? That's perfect. They want the full landscape.

For others — especially the ones who already did the research, already know the context, just need someone to CUT THROUGH IT — it's infuriating.

The fix isn't a better prompt. It's a profile.

When AI knows you're a decisive, bottom-line thinker, it stops performing thoroughness and starts respecting your time.

"Give me the one thing I should do" becomes an instruction it already understands about you — before you even ask.

That's the difference between AI that works and AI that performs.

Which type are you: give-me-everything or just-tell-me-what-to-do? 👇
